programme.title: The Blue Eyed Butcher Case
programme.desc: A longtime friend of Jeff and Susan Wright testified for the defense. Susan's legal team wanted to give a different perspective to who exactly Jeff was, which differed from the state's view of him. This friend told the jury the time she saw the defendant with a black eye allegedly endured at the hands of her husband. This went into the numerous domestic issues that occurred during their rocky four-year marriage.

programme.title: The Blue Eyed Butcher Case
programme.desc: During their case-in-chief Susan Wright's Defense brought up separate domestic incidents between her and Jeff, her husband. With friends on the witness stand jurors learned of Jeff, The Angry, abusive husband who physically hurt Susan and their young son. Just get a divorce. That's what the prosecution brought up under cross-examination of the defendant's close friend.

programme.title: The Blue Eyed Butcher Case
programme.desc: Susan Wright's attorneys called her mother to the stand to give more insight into the drama of her marriage to Jeff. The Defense wanted to show how much of a victim of domestic abuse the defendant was in the months and weeks before the murder. Sue Wyche testified that at one point Susan moved out of her home with her allegedly abusive husband, Jeff, and then moved in with her parents. The reason - the constant physical, mental, and emotional abuse.

programme.desc: In 1964, civil rights workers who set out to educate Southern Black people about the right to vote disappeared in Mississippi. After they were found lynched, the FBI gained the trust of an informant who led to the conviction of local KKK members.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Vince Gigante, the reputed boss of the Genovese crime family, wandered the streets of New York wearing only pajamas and a bathrobe, but the FBI was convinced he was anything but crazy.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: When an 80-year-old woman is found barely conscious next to the body of her murdered son in an abandoned van, the FBI was brought in and linked the crime to a teenager and middle-aged man.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Chicago police and the FBI began to suspect that a law enforcement officer was responsible for the crimes.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In New York, an armored car robbery left one guard dead and another critically wounded. The FBI suspected domestic terrorists with a history of violent crime. Their investigation led them to the Black Liberation Army and the Weather Underground.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Drug gangs terrorized Harlem, New York neighborhoods for years in the early 1990s. After a 10-year-old boy became a crossfire victim, NYPD detectives and the FBI designed an elite task forced called C-11 to dismantle the gang.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: The body of an unidentified young woman turned up on a highway in a small Pennsylvania town. Agents persisted, eventually identifying the body and finding the killer.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In Arizona, a shipment of money vanished without a trace, along with the two men hired to guard it. Agents didn't know if the guards were willing participants or victims of an ambush. Suspicions grew that the crime was an inside job.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: This special episode examines previous cases to learn about the investigative techniques used by the FBI.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: Six Black men had been brutally murdered; two had their hearts cut out. The only thing connecting the victims was their ethnicity and the fact that each had been shot with .22 caliber bullets.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A class field trip ended with an 11-year-old girl missing. When the parents failed to locate their daughter in their quiet Illinois town, the FBI joined the investigation, working with friends and family in an urgent search to find the missing girl.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In June 1973, a young girl was abducted from a state park while camping with her family. A criminal profiler predicted that the kidnapper would contact the girl's family on the one-year anniversary of her abduction.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: A drug dealer engaged in a shootout with Nebraska state troopers and evaded capture after a high-speed chase.

programme.title: The FBI Files
programme.desc: In 1990s Philadelphia, a ruthless grab for power led to a full-scale mob war. Using informants and high-tech surveillance, the FBI infiltrated the most powerful mob family in the city and sought to bring down the organization.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Teen Mhairi was killed on her walk home after a festive night. Investigators traced her last steps, using CCTV and forensics that pointed to Gavin, a violent repeat offender whose history revealed how dangerous he truly was.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: In October 2004, Rachel O'Reilly left her home to drop her children off at school. Less than 20 minutes after returning home she was murdered. Her husband, Joe O'Reilly, staged the crime scene to make it look like a burglary gone wrong.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Two young mothers vanished from East London's streets. At first unconnected, the cases came together when evidence found in Derek's home showed both women suffered the same violent fate at his hands.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of one of the most prolific serial killers in Canadian history, dubbed The Pig Farmer Killer.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The Bayou Strangler deceived, bound, assaulted and murdered men, as he drifted across South Louisiana.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Dayton Leroy Rogers, known as The Molalla Forest Killer, mutilated and killed at least eight women in the US.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Shore was a dangerous sexual predator who will be remembered as one of the world's most evil killers.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The chilling story of the serial killer who worked as a nurse in Texas, where she killed many children and infants by injecting them with drugs.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The chilling story of Mark Martin, who murdered three homeless women in his quest to become Nottingham's first serial killer.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of the Baton Rouge Serial Killer, known for murdering seven women in the area and sentenced to death for his chilling crimes.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of the Beltway sniper attacks. John Allen and his teenage accomplice carried out the brutal series of shootings that killed ten.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of Malcolm Green, who was jailed for life for the brutal murder of a Cardiff prostitute, only to kill again when he was released after 18 years.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Catherine Nevin and her husband Tom were successful publicans and property developers in the Dublin and Wicklow areas. Catherine reported a break-in and violent attack at their pub one night in 1996, which left Tom dead.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Steven Dean Gordon and Frank Cano murdered at least four sex workers in California between 2012 and 2013.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: He portrayed himself as a pillar of the community, but the story of this cult killer was deeply sinister.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: The story of the killer who took four lives during a chilling two-day killing spree in 2004 after being inspired by horror films.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Laura disappeared without warning after years with Hassan, whom she met abroad. Detectives dug into her life and uncovered troubling details about her marriage, eventually revealing the devastating truth of her fate.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: Ali Qazimaj is an Albanian conman who brutally murdered an elderly couple in 2016.

programme.title: World's Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: She became the first woman to be executed by lethal injection after taking six lives. What drove her to commit these callous murders?

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a decomposed body is found stuffed in a barrel, it's easy to presume murder. One of the few clues was an address book found along with the body.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Shortly after daybreak in Vancouver, British Columbia, a fire was set in a dumpster. No one saw either the arsonist or the fire, and it burned for hours in the deserted parking lot.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman was shot to death in her Connecticut driveway. Now police must determine if love had turned deadly.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a wealthy real estate tycoon goes missing, it appears to be foul play. In his will, he leaves instructions in case he should die under violent circumstances - which were carried out after a hiker comes across his bullet-ridden skull.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A beautiful young woman falls to her death from a cliff overlooking the ocean. Initially, investigators think it was a tragic accident. But after a forensic examination, police uncover a very different picture of what happened.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Two men were murdered in their beds. The next night, arson destroyed a family planning clinic. Investigators find a link between the two separate crimes.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Heavy rains and snow run-off carried manure into Milwaukee's water supply in 1993, leading to an epidemic of gastrointestinal illness affecting 400,000 people.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When Genell Plude is found dead in her bathroom, the scene points to suicide. But a coroner's inquest and a unique application of forensic science gave investigators a different explanation for her death.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In the summer of 1986, Kathleen Lipscomb's body was found on a deserted road outside of San Antonio. Months passed, then years, and the crime was never solved.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Stephen Scher and Martin Dillon went skeet shooting on a beautiful spring day and Martin was shot dead.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A murder investigation in Florida crosses jurisdictions from New York and Jamaica. The police rely on cell phone mapping, wiretapping and a host of forensic evidence to link a suspect to a murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2000, Judy Southern arrived home from work and was shot by a gunman waiting within. Her husband Allen arrived shortly afterwards, called 911 to report his wife had been shot.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A computer crash pushed a manufacturing company to the brink of collapse. Investigators must determine if the crash was the result of a sabotage.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman convicted of killing her mother in a car fire is granted a new trial. An expert witness has a different opinion as to how the fire started and, if proven, would clear the woman of any culpability.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A wealthy man and his wife were attacked by three masked men outside their luxurious Louisiana home. He was shot dead and she was forced to open their hidden safe.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1999, a 12-year-old was found murdered in her home in Minnesota. Despite a meticulous search of the home and an exhaustive investigation, police had no suspects.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Paul Gruber's daughter suspected something was wrong when she received birthday cards addressed from her father, but written in a stranger's handwriting.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When 19-year-old Lori Auker left her Pennsylvania home for work and never arrived, it took space-age technology, cat hairs and insects to pinpoint the image of the woman's abductor.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The body of a college co-ed was found on a farm covered with her own denim as well as the jeans of the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: After the murder of Seattle residents, evidence from the couple's dog eventually ties a murderer to the scene.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Dianna Green was brutally attacked in her own home and her unborn child was killed. After coming out of a coma and regaining her memory, she is able to identify her husband as the perpetrator.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A teenager went missing after an evening of horseback riding. Her body was found a month later, three miles from her home. The killer unknowingly left trace evidence behind - tiny but unmistakable clues which pointed to him and him alone.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The body of an attractive young woman was found a mile from her abandoned car. Police were especially concerned when they realized the victim had come to them for protection just two weeks earlier.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police are dispatched to the scene of a shooting, and discover the victim's husband is one of their own: a homicide detective who says his wife accidentally shot herself in the head.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A Colorado hunter is the victim of a well-planned crime. Investigators suspect two weapons and two killers.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In rural Canada, Dr. John Schneeberger is accused of sedating and sexually assaulting one of his female patients and his stepdaughter. DNA tests demonstrate that the doctor is innocent, but the patient insists that he sedated and raped her.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man's wife took her own life and his college sweetheart killed herself the same way. Was it coincidence?

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man who had committed crimes including murder was not caught. Almost 50 years later, advances in technology and handwriting analysis uncovered the criminal.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Authorities track a kidnapper who let his victim write a last will and informed the family of her murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When police find the body of an 11-year-old, there are few clues to the killer's identity. But authorities are intrigued by unusual orange fibers embedded in clothing found near the victim.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Detectives search for the bombers of two churches in Illinois, hoping that the materials used in the remnants of the handmade bombs will offer the clues to catch the culprits.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An obstetrician returned home from the hospital and found his wife on the bathroom floor. She was covered with blood and not breathing. He tried unsuccessfully to revive her, staining his clothes with her blood in the process, and then he called 911.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A two-year-old boy was rushed to the hospital suffering from brain seizures and breathing difficulties.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A serial rapist is on the loose in a Texas town. No one knew where he'd strike next, but the audacity of his attacks and the escalation of violence against his victims made finding him imperative.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An investigation of a pediatrician, prompted by the death of one of her patients, leads to a Texas hospital where the pediatric mortality rate is higher than at any hospital in the country.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Nancy Newman and her two daughters are found brutally murdered. The crime scene has no shortage of evidence.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: For six years, a serial killer prowled NYC. He wrote letters to police indicating he would kill twelve people.
